title: AMD presents its Helios system with the Instinct MI455X accelerator to compete with Nvidia in data centers
summary: The new rack system integrates the MI455X accelerator with 432 GB of HBM4 and partners with Cerebras for low-latency AI inference.

title: Leading AI companies accumulate hidden debt of $1.65 trillion
summary: Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, Microsoft, and Oracle reportedly have undisclosed liabilities that raise questions about the sustainability of artificial intelligence spending.

title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT Health amid lawsuit over dangerous medical advice
summary: The ChatGPT health service is rolling out globally while a court evaluates a damages lawsuit and a study indicates the model worsens without payment.

title: Google allows logging in with a selfie video to verify identity
summary: The company adds the option of biometric verification via selfie to access accounts and regain access.

title: Microsoft introduces Xbox backward compatibility on PC with four classic games
summary: Original Xbox titles can be played on PC via an Xbox 360 emulator, according to early tests.

title: Nvidia raises prices on its GDDR7 and GDDR6 memory kits, making GeForce GPUs more expensive
summary: The increase in memory cost will affect the final prices of graphics cards, according to several manufacturers' reports.

title: Patreon lays off 20% of its workforce as part of a restructuring
summary: The subscription platform announces a staff adjustment affecting 80 employees, justified as a necessary move for sustainability.

title: Meta publishes optimistic AI ad with a song about the end of the world
summary: The company launches an advertising campaign using the song 'It's the End of the World as We Know It' to promote its artificial intelligence.

title: Microsoft tests free Xbox Cloud Gaming with ads and one-hour sessions
summary: The ad-supported free version of Microsoft's cloud gaming service takes its first steps in a testing phase.

title: Sony plans to incorporate magnetic anti-drift sticks in the PlayStation 6 controller
summary: Future console controllers would use TMR sensors to avoid the joystick drift problem, according to leaks.

title: Valve makes it easier to gift games on Steam by allowing purchases without a guest account
summary: Users can purchase games as gifts for others without needing a Steam account, simplifying the process.

title: Augmental launches MouthPad, a buccal trackpad controlled with the tongue via Bluetooth
summary: The device allows controlling smartphones, tablets, and PCs through tongue movements, offering an accessibility alternative.

title: Geekbench 7 launches with new CPU, AI, and media tests, and a Ryzen 7 7700 baseline
summary: The new version of the benchmark introduces physics, photo editing, and AV1 tests, as well as CUDA compatibility and a new reference point.

title: AMD introduces the EPYC Venice family with Zen 6 cores and 3D V-Cache versions
summary: The new server processors offer up to 256 cores, 1,152 MB of L3 cache, and frequencies above 5 GHz to compete in high-performance computing.

title: AMD launches Ryzen AI Embedded X100 (Strix Halo) APUs for robotics and physical AI
summary: The new chips combine up to 16 Zen 5 cores with RDNA 3.5 GPU to compete with Intel Panther Lake in embedded systems.

title: Intel and AMD sign long-term agreements with Chinese customers as CPU prices rise more than 40%
summary: Chip manufacturers guarantee purchase volumes with Chinese customers for one year amid price escalation and geopolitical tension.

title: Growing public opposition to AI data centers with protests in 42 US states
summary: Residents of several communities mobilize against the construction of AI infrastructure, denouncing its impact on the land and lack of control.

title: Tech entrepreneurs ask not to block Chinese AI models and Jensen Huang advocates using them
summary: Startup founders and Nvidia's CEO defend access to Chinese open-weight models, while the US government debates restrictions.
